name: Ragi Dosa
highlight: Instant batter — no fermentation needed, ready in 20 minutes

hero.alt: Crisp brown ragi dosa rolled on a banana leaf with coconut chutney
hero.caption: ragi dosa — finger-millet crepe, lacy edges

healthFacts: Ragi (finger millet) has 344mg calcium per 100g — the highest of any cereal grain.
Slow-digesting carbs and high fiber keep glucose flat for 3+ hours after the meal.
Naturally gluten-free; rich in manganese, magnesium, and phenolic antioxidants.
Iron content (~3.9mg/100g) supports hemoglobin without animal sources.

steps.title: Mix the pourable batter
steps.detail: Whisk both flours with 2.5 cups water, salt, and cumin until lump-free. Stir in onion, green chili, and curry leaves. The consistency should be thinner than pancake batter.
steps.tip: Thin batter = lacy, crispy dosa. Stir before each pour.

steps.title: Pour and crisp
steps.detail: Heat a flat tawa on medium-high. Pour a ladleful of batter from the edges inward — it should spread on its own. Drizzle ½ tsp oil around the edge. Cook 2 minutes until edges lift and the underside is brown and lacy.
steps.tip: Don't try to spread it with a ladle — pour-and-let-it-fall is the technique.

steps.title: Fold and serve
steps.detail: Loosen with a spatula and fold in half. Serve hot with coconut chutney or a thick yogurt dip. Repeat for the rest of the batter.
steps.tip: Wipe the tawa with a halved onion between dosas — keeps it non-stick.
